title: Bridge Essay: The two-step task, avoidance, and OCD.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Mechanicality** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Mechanicality — habit as automated behavior, loss of conscious control, automaticity.

**The two-step task, avoidance, and OCD.**. J Neurosci Res.. PMID: 32282095. DOI: 10.1002/jnr.24594. In this invited review, we argue for the need to determine whether appetitive and aversive behaviors, be they goal-directed or habitual, share overlapping neural circuitry. To motivate our argument, we first summarize what is currently known about the neural circuits governing aversive and appetitive behaviors by focusing first on the three hypothesized phases of avoidance learning, and then on goal-directed and habitual reward seeking.
